subject: Gaming
title: Preservation: Saving Gaming History
description: A 60-second lesson on Preservation: Saving Gaming History.

hook.text: 87% of classic games are unavailable for legal purchase. Gaming history is disappearing faster than any other medium.

buildup.text: Old consoles break. Servers shut down. Digital stores close. Games vanish when companies stop supporting them.

discovery.text: The Video Game History Foundation fights to preserve games. ROM archives and emulation keep classics accessible.

twist.text: Companies often oppose preservation. Nintendo sues ROM sites even for games they no longer sell.

climax.text: Without active preservation, most gaming history will be lost within decades. The clock is ticking.

punchline.text: Eighty-seven percent unavailable. Gaming history is vanishing right now.

quiz.question: What percentage of classic games are unavailable for legal purchase?
